channel.title: KillBait RSS Feed
channel.description: KillBait - News highlights delivered clearly and responsibly—no clickbait, no sensationalism

item.title: Bishop Calls for Prayer and Responsible Voting During Economic Challenges
item.description: The Bishop of the Methodist Church Nigeria, Kono Diocese, Rt. Rev. Menele Kenneth Nwibiabubari, don call on Christians to continue dey pray well well even as di economy dey tough for Nigeria. Di call ...

item.title: MPs to vote on whether Starmer misled Parliament over Mandelson appointment
item.description: Members of Parliament are set to vote on whether Prime Minister Sir Keir Starmer should face an investigation for allegedly misleading the House of Commons regarding the appointment of Lord Mandelson ...

item.title: Nigeria Minister Responds to Ruto on English Language Remarks
item.description: During the Mining Investment Conference and Expo in Nairobi on Tuesday, Nigeria's Minister of Solid Minerals Development, Henry Dele Alake, made a light-hearted but pointed comment directed at Preside...

item.title: Gachagua Drops Bid for Reinstatement as Deputy President, Seeks Compensation at Milimani Courts
item.description: Former Deputy President Rigathi Gachagua together with his wife Pastor Dorcas and their two sons arrived at the Milimani Law Courts in Nairobi in a flashy 2024 Range Rover Vogue. The family made a sty...

item.title: Germany's Military and Foreign Policy Shifts Since Reunification
item.description: The article analyzes Germany's post-reunification military and foreign policy trajectory, arguing that the country has resumed assertive military behavior since regaining sovereignty in 1990. The auth...

item.title: Prof Roselyn Konya Calls on Methodist Church Members to Engage in 2027 Elections
item.description: Prof Roselyn Konya, former Rivers State Commissioner for Environment, don encourage members of Methodist Church Nigeria make dem no siddon look, but dey participate actively for the 2027 general elect...

item.title: Tanzania and Slovakia to deepen business and industrial cooperation
item.description: Tanzania and Slovakia have decided to step up their collaboration in trade, industrial growth, and investment by encouraging direct business-to-business interactions. The agreement came on April 27, 2...

item.title: AI quietly supports South Africa’s expanding online gaming platforms
item.description: Artificial intelligence is steadily making its way into South Africa’s digital services, particularly in the online gaming sector, without drawing much attention. Rather than altering core game mechan...

item.title: Kwale County Strengthens Trade Between Kenya and Tanzania
item.description: State Department of East African Community Affairs Principal Secretary Caroline Karugu has highlighted Kwale County as a key point for trade between Kenya and Tanzania. The Lungalunga One Stop Border ...

item.title: Czech authorities clarify no extradition case against former Congressman Zaldy Co
item.description: The Czech Ministry of Justice has clarified that there are currently no extradition proceedings against former Ako Bicol party-list congressman Elizaldy “Zaldy” Co. The statement came after a meeting ...

item.title: Philippine and US Militaries Conduct Joint Counter-Landing Exercise in Palawan
item.description: On April 27, 2026, the Philippine and US militaries conducted a Counter-Landing Live Fire Exercise at Apurawan Beach in Aborlan, Palawan, as part of the annual Balikatan 2026 joint military exercises....

item.title: Tanzania Education Stakeholders Call for Balanced Timetables Integrating Theory and Practical Training
item.description: In Dodoma, education stakeholders gathered for a capacity building session organised by the National Timetablers Forum to discuss how to improve academic schedules. The experts emphasised the need to ...

item.title: Cayetano Explains Bato's Senate Absence Amid ICC Concerns
item.description: Senator Alan Peter Cayetano clarified the situation surrounding Senator Ronald 'Bato' dela Rosa's continued absence from the Senate since November 2025. According to Cayetano, dela Rosa is willing and...

item.title: FG Introduces Multi-Lingual Toolkits to Help Nigerian SMEs Trade Across Africa
item.description: On Monday, the Federal Government of Nigeria waka enter new step to make sure say the country fit take full advantage of the African Continental Free Trade Area (AfCFTA). Dr. Jumoke Oduwole, Minister ...

item.title: Northern Ireland police reinforce security after attempted car bomb attack in Dunmurry
item.description: Police in Northern Ireland have intensified patrols and established additional vehicle checkpoints following a car bomb attack outside the Dunmurry Police Service of Northern Ireland (PSNI) station. T...

item.title: HR leaders advise teens on skills for an AI-driven workplace
item.description: At The Australian Financial Review Workforce Summit, business leaders shared their perspectives on preparing young people for the AI-driven workforce. The discussion highlighted the importance of blen...

item.title: Kerry and Ryan Stokes Appoint Rohan Lund as CEO of Merged Southern Cross and Seven
item.description: Kerry and Ryan Stokes have strategically positioned Rohan Lund as the chief executive of the newly merged Southern Cross and Seven. Lund, who previously spent a decade leading the NRMA, is recognised ...

item.title: Police Clash with Khyber Tribesmen During Protest Over Quadcopter Strike
item.description: Early on Tuesday, police in Peshawar reportedly used firing and tear gas to disperse around 150 tribesmen from Khyber district protesting a suspected quadcopter strike in Bara’s Akkakhel area that kil...

item.title: Who Wants to Be a Millionaire: Seven Winners and Their £1 Million Questions
item.description: Since its debut in 1998, the UK version of Who Wants to Be a Millionaire has seen only seven contestants correctly answer all fifteen questions to win the £1 million jackpot. The latest winner, Roman ...

item.title: Judicial Commission Approves Transfer of Justice Mohsin Kayani from IHC to LHC
item.description: The Judicial Commission of Pakistan (JCP), in a meeting chaired by Chief Justice Yahya Afridi, has approved the transfer of Justice Mohsin Akhtar Kayani from Islamabad High Court (IHC) to Lahore High ...
